content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a light/energy reflection *and* a deliberate cognitive act of reviewing past experience, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to serve two complementary functions simultaneously: bouncing back inconsistent perspective signals (analogous to physical reflection) to expose flawed reasoning, and consolidating those signals into targeted updates to the agent's dynamic long-term memory (analogous to cognitive reflection). This dual-function design draws directly on the dual semantic roots of the word 'reflexion' that are rarely explicitly leveraged in AI agent architecture design.
